Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Users Request Input A description of an insurance claim management process with specific steps and loops Output Python code to construct an accepting Petri net using pm4py libraries Requirements Use PetriNet Marking and petri_utils from pm4py Define places for states between activities Define transitions labeled with activity names Connect them with arcs to form a logical sequence Include loops for re submission DV RDS and investigation FC II Define initial marking im with a token in a start place Define final marking fm with a token in an end place after Closure Provide complete runnable Python code

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Deconstruct the Process Flow Lets map the steps to transitions and places Start to CR CR to DV DV if incomplete to RDS back to DV DV if complete to FC FC if doubts to II back to FC FC if clear to CA CA to AP AP to P P to N N to CL to End

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I need to model the choices loops explicitly with places and transitions In Petri nets choices loops are typically modeled with places that split merge flows

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait in a standard Petri net to model a choice loop I need a place that splits Actually the loop can be modeled as DV to p dv done p dv done to RDS to p rds done to DV and p dv done to FC

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For simplicity in PM4Py process mining contexts we often just connect them sequentially with a loop back Actually a simpler way that matches typical process mining Petri nets is DV to p dv out p dv out to RDS to p rds out to DV and p dv out to FC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: A cleaner way start to CR to p cr p cr to DV to p dv p dv to RDS to p rds to DV loop p dv to FC to p fc p fc to II to p ii to FC loop p fc to CA to p ca p ca to AP to p ap p ap to P to p p p p to N to p n p n to CL to sink

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This correctly models the described process with loops

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Map to PM4Py API I need to use PetriNet name Marking PetriNet Place name PetriNet Transition name label net places add net transitions add petri_utils add_arc_from_to source target net Marking im source equals 1 fm sink equals 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Lets draft the code step by step from pm4py objects petri net obj import PetriNet Marking from pm4py objects petri net utils import petri_utils net equals PetriNet name InsuranceClaimProcess followed by places transitions additions arcs and markings

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check for Correctness and PM4Py Conventions PetriNet Place and PetriNet Transition are correctly instantiated net places add and net transitions add work petri_utils add_arc_from_to is correct Marking is a dict like object im source equals 1 works The loops are correctly modeled

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: One potential issue p dv done has two outgoing arcs If RDS fires it consumes the token If FC fires it also consumes the token This is a standard XOR split in Petri nets Its acceptable

Name: Inductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Ill add comments explaining the structure Ill wrap it in a clean readable script I should also make sure the code is self contained and ready to run

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Verification Check PM4Py API petri_utils add_arc_from_to source target net is correct The loop structure is correct Is there any risk of deadlock No assuming the process follows the intended path Everything matches
